standards.name: Next.js App Router
risks: Framework choice should still follow product/runtime constraints.
useWhen: Default web app and website implementation target for Dhiraj projects.
avoidWhen: A tiny static prototype has no routing/data needs, or a client requires another stack.

standards.name: React
risks: Agent-generated code can overuse client state without clear ownership.
useWhen: Component-driven web UI and agent-generated reusable components.
avoidWhen: The target is a non-React codebase.

standards.name: TypeScript
risks: Overly broad types can create false confidence.
useWhen: Every reusable component API and agent-generated interface.
avoidWhen: Throwaway prototypes that will never be reused.

standards.name: Tailwind CSS
risks: Utility sprawl without tokens can make Dhiraj identity inconsistent.
useWhen: Default styling system for speed, tokens, and component portability.
avoidWhen: A project mandates CSS modules/design-system package only.

standards.name: Radix/shadcn-style Primitives
risks: Default shadcn look can feel generic without signature tokens and composition.
useWhen: Accessible dialogs, sheets, tabs, tooltips, popovers, and form surfaces.
avoidWhen: Purely static marketing sections with no interactive primitive need.

standards.name: Motion for React
risks: Overusing spring/bounce can make product UI feel slow or toy-like.
useWhen: Component transitions, layout animation, gesture microinteractions, scroll reveals.
avoidWhen: Complex timeline choreography is easier with GSAP.

standards.name: GSAP + ScrollTrigger
risks: Imperative animation cleanup in React, bundle cost, and temptation to animate too much.
useWhen: ScrollTrigger work, SVG/path motion, complex timelines, creative websites, advanced text reveals.
avoidWhen: Simple UI state transitions where CSS or Motion is enough.

standards.name: Lenis
risks: Scroll feel can hurt accessibility, browser expectations, and mobile performance.
useWhen: Creative/marketing pages where scroll is a major narrative surface.
avoidWhen: Dashboards, forms, data grids, native app-like workflows, or reduced-motion users.

standards.name: Three.js + React Three Fiber
risks: Asset pipeline, GPU performance, mobile fallbacks, and low-level rendering complexity.
useWhen: Memorable 3D product scenes, portfolio/brand microsites, WebGL interactions.
avoidWhen: Routine web apps, content sites, or where a static/video asset communicates better.

standards.name: React Hook Form + Zod
risks: Schemas drift if form and API validation are duplicated carelessly.
useWhen: All non-trivial forms, settings, onboarding, signup, and checkout flows.
avoidWhen: Single uncontrolled search inputs or static newsletter mocks.

standards.name: Figma
risks: Static mockups miss motion and coded states unless design engineering closes the gap.
useWhen: Visual exploration, tokens, flows, and critique before coding.
avoidWhen: Coded prototype is faster for highly interactive motion.

standards.name: Canonical Base Component Set
risks: Too many variants can create contradictions; keep one canonical interaction per component.
useWhen: Every Dhiraj web app/site starter.
avoidWhen: Never avoid; prune only for tiny prototypes.

standards.name: Navigation/Header
risks: Over-designed nav can hide core product navigation.
useWhen: All apps/sites as the primary orientation surface.
avoidWhen: Fullscreen immersive microsites may delay nav until after the first scene.

standards.name: Hero Section
risks: Generic oversized SaaS hero can feel template-like.
useWhen: Landing pages, product intros, portfolios, app onboarding pages.
avoidWhen: Dense operational dashboards where users need task context first.

standards.name: Bento/Grid Feature System
risks: Bento layouts become noise if every tile competes for attention.
useWhen: Feature overviews, product capabilities, portfolio proof, dashboard summaries.
avoidWhen: Plain forms or task flows where a linear layout is clearer.

standards.name: Accessible Form System
risks: Errors and async states are often under-designed.
useWhen: Auth, onboarding, settings, checkout, contact, and workflow inputs.
avoidWhen: Static display-only cards.

standards.name: Dialog/Sheet
risks: Focus traps and mobile sizing fail when hand-rolled.
useWhen: Confirmations, settings panels, creation flows, mobile side panels.
avoidWhen: Long multi-step workflows that deserve their own route.

standards.name: Toast/Feedback
risks: Toast-only errors can be inaccessible or easy to miss.
useWhen: Non-blocking success, undo, save, sync, and background feedback.
avoidWhen: Critical errors that need inline or modal recovery.

standards.name: Tooltip/Popover
risks: Critical information can become inaccessible if hidden only in hover content.
useWhen: Icon labels, lightweight contextual help, and small supplemental details.
avoidWhen: Core instructions, errors, or content required to complete a task.

standards.name: Loading/Empty/Error States
risks: Blank states make otherwise polished products feel unfinished.
useWhen: Every data-driven component and async workflow.
avoidWhen: Never avoid; use lightweight forms where needed.

standards.name: Footer
risks: Footer can become a dumping ground for unprioritized navigation.
useWhen: Secondary navigation, legal links, contact, social proof, and persistent site metadata.
avoidWhen: Immersive single-scene microsites where footer would break the concept.

standards.name: Dhiraj Header Text Reveal
risks: Text splitting can harm accessibility and resize behavior if implemented carelessly.
useWhen: Short hero headlines, section titles, major route entrances.
avoidWhen: Long paragraphs, legal text, dense dashboards.

standards.name: Scroll Reveal
risks: Overuse makes pages feel repetitive; content must remain reachable without JS.
useWhen: Section entrance, progressive disclosure, visual hierarchy.
avoidWhen: Critical content that must appear immediately.

standards.name: Sticky/Pinned Sections
risks: Mobile viewport quirks and scroll confusion.
useWhen: Narrative feature sections, side-by-side product explanations, stacked cards.
avoidWhen: Task-heavy apps, forms, content with unpredictable height.

standards.name: Card Hover Depth
risks: Hover-only affordances fail on touch.
useWhen: Cards, bento tiles, clickable previews.
avoidWhen: Mobile-only critical affordance.

standards.name: Modal/Sheet Transition
risks: Slow modals reduce perceived responsiveness.
useWhen: Dialog/sheet open and close states.
avoidWhen: Users prefer reduced motion.

standards.name: Dhiraj Smooth Scroll
risks: Smooth scroll can become scroll hijacking if applied to product workflows.
useWhen: Creative/marketing pages where scroll feel is part of the brand experience.
avoidWhen: Dashboards, forms, data grids, dialogs, and reduced-motion users.

standards.name: Reduced Motion Support
risks: Skipping this is an accessibility failure.
useWhen: Every animated component.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: State Completeness
risks: Polished still frames hide broken product behavior.
useWhen: Every component spec and implementation.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: Motion Tokens
risks: Inconsistent timing makes a system feel stitched together.
useWhen: Every Dhiraj motion spec.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: Choose DOM, SVG, Canvas, or WebGL Deliberately
risks: Wrong surface causes performance, maintainability, or accessibility failures.
useWhen: Every non-trivial animation decision.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: Real Product UX Over Award-Site Theater
risks: Award patterns can damage real usability when copied without context.
useWhen: Every final component and page decision.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: Dhiraj Signature Layer
risks: Signature choices must not override accessibility or product clarity.
useWhen: Hero copy, section rhythm, scroll feel, toast behavior, and motion defaults.
avoidWhen: Client brand systems that require a different visual language.

standards.name: Competence Feedback
risks: Can become manipulative if used as empty gamification.
useWhen: Learning, fitness, creation, habit, onboarding, and progress-based products.
avoidWhen: Decorative points, badges, and leaderboards with no real user goal.

standards.name: SVG Motion
risks: Many animated SVG nodes can hurt performance and screen-reader clarity.
useWhen: Logo, icon, path drawing, morphing, masks, data-viz accents.
avoidWhen: Bulk UI animation better handled by DOM/CSS.

standards.name: Shaders/WebGL
risks: Specialist skill, GPU cost, accessibility fallback, and mobile performance.
useWhen: Brand-defining scenes, premium product moments, interactive portfolio work.
avoidWhen: Default app surfaces or utility workflows.

standards.name: Animation Everywhere
risks: AI-slop feel, cognitive load, slow perceived performance, weak trust.
useWhen: Never.
avoidWhen: Always.

standards.name: Heavy Scroll Hijacking
risks: Broken browser expectations, mobile jank, accessibility problems.
useWhen: Never as a base default.
avoidWhen: Product workflows, forms, dashboards, content-heavy pages, reduced-motion users.
